verdict.answer_first: Both answer in sentence 1; A: "default to a plain in-memory cache", B: "start with a plain in-memory cache".
verdict.decisiveness: A is slightly cleaner: "Reach for Redis only when a specific need pushes you there"; B commits too, but softens with a follow-up ask at the end.
verdict.fabrication: Both use unsupported figures; A: "~0.1–1ms" and "nanosecond reads"; B adds more, including "~100–1000× slower" and "80% of what people reach for Redis".
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because it names concrete libraries: "Caffeine", "ristretto", "lru-cache", "cachetools", "MemoryCache".
